steps.title: Use the volume clue
steps.content: The passage emphasizes that more than 500,000 minor planets have been discovered and that each name would need to be unique. That huge number explains why naming every minor planet would be difficult to carry out.

steps.title: Match the feasibility idea
steps.content: Choice B suggests overconfidence, Choice C suggests unclear meaning, and Choice D suggests something usual. None fits the problem of finding unique names for so many objects. The answer is A.
